steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content:
For a linear equation in one variable to have no solution, the $x$-coefficients must match while the constants differ. So first expand the left side:
$$6(kx-n)=6kx-6n$$

steps.title: Check the no-solution condition
steps.content: With $k=-\frac{29}{30}$, the $x$-terms cancel. The constants are $-6n$ and $-\frac{31}{7}$. Since $n\gt 1$, $6n\gt 6\gt \frac{31}{7}$, so $-6n\neq-\frac{31}{7}$. The remaining statement is a contradiction, so no solution exists. Equivalent accepted answers are -29/30, -0.966, and -0.967.
